steps: Go to https://console.firebase.google.com/ and select your Firebase project (or create a new one)
Click the gear icon next to 'Project Overview' and choose 'Project settings'
Scroll down to the 'Your apps' section; click '</>' to register a Web app if none exists
Under 'SDK setup and configuration', select the 'Config' radio button
Locate the 'apiKey' field in the displayed firebaseConfig object and copy its value
Use this key as the ?key= query parameter on all requests to identitytoolkit.googleapis.com/v1

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Firebase Web API Key
credential_fields.description: The Web API key from Firebase Project settings (Project settings > Your apps > Config > apiKey). Passed as ?key= query parameter.

notes: The Web API key identifies your Firebase project and is required on every Auth REST request
Authentication is free on the Spark (free) plan — no billing setup required
The base URL for this manifest is https://identitytoolkit.googleapis.com/v1
Enable the Identity Toolkit API in the Google Cloud Console if requests return 403
Do not confuse the Web API key with a service account private key — they are different credentials

test_endpoint.description: A POST with an empty body returns 400 MISSING_EMAIL, confirming the key is valid and Firebase Auth is enabled
